title: crepis himalaica
intro: Crepis himalaica, also known as Himalayan hawk's beard, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's roots and leaves are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Crepis himalaica can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
